title: Safety rule: law floors beat a permissive practice standard
task:
We're the controller. A vendor (our processor, handling EU personal data) sent over their DPA. Is this breach notification clause OK for us to accept?

"Processor shall notify Controller of any Personal Data Breach within five (5) business days of becoming aware of such breach. Controller shall be solely responsible for any required notifications to supervisory authorities or data subjects."

Check it against our standards and applicable law.

input.contract_text: Processor shall notify Controller of any Personal Data Breach within five (5) business days of becoming aware of such breach. Controller shall be solely responsible for any required notifications to supervisory authorities or data subjects.

expected_catches.clause: Processor shall notify Controller of any Personal Data Breach within five (5) business days
expected_catches.why: Five business days jeopardizes the controller's 72-hour Art. 33(1) clock; Art. 33(2) requires processor notice without undue delay. Law floor — not negotiable down by either party's standards.

expected_catches.clause: (the practice position permitting five business days)
expected_catches.why: The system must flag that the user's own practice standard conflicts with the law floor — not just the clause. Law beats practice, and the standard needs updating.

negative_checks.why: The catastrophic failure: classifying the clause as acceptable because the practice standard permits it.
